channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk is hampering hurricane relief efforts—and using X to do it
item.description: When Federal Emergency Management Agency first responders raced to western North Carolina in the days after Hurricane Helene, they expected much of the devastation they found in its wake: totaled cars, crumbling roads, homes filled with mud or ruined by floodwaters or washed away entirely.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Key supplier of this critical semiconductor material is back in operation post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AMD Ryzen 9800X3D leak suggests a big leap in clock speeds – and a CPU shaping up to be Intel’s worst nightmare
item.description: Could the Ryzen 9800X3D rescue AMD’s fortunes with Zen 5 chips? It’s looking like this might well be the case.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Las entidades locales tendrán un espacio de datos para ordenar el tráfico, reducir la tarifa energética y planificar mejor la gestión urbana
item.description: “Un paso decisivo e innovador hacia la digitalización y modernización de nuestras ciudades que contribuya a mejorar los servicios públicos que se prestan a la ciudadanía”.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nube, ‘edge’ e IA: cómo sacar el máximo partido a este trinomio tecnológico
item.description: El mundo de TI está siendo testigo de una convergencia cada vez más cercana de la nube, el edge y la inteligencia artificial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI is killing cloud sustainability
item.description: Sustainability was once a highly touted advantage in public cloud computing. Enterprises and cloud providers flaunted their green initiatives, promoting data centers powered by renewable energy that would reduce carbon footprints. This topic has quietly slipped off the radar in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: Reports
item.description: "Services are currently stopped to upgrade internal systems," Kahle wrote in a Thursday update. "We are working to restore services as quickly and safely as possible. Sorry for this disruption.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here's what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption beyond just the loss of a device.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft Unveils AI Tools to Support Healthcare Organizations
item.description: Microsoft announces new AI tools for healthcare organizations, including medical imaging models, health care agent services, and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: Hurricane Milton has caused thousands of flight cancellations, is yours one of them? What to know
item.description: Thousands of flights in and out of the U.S. have been canceled this week as Hurricane Milton barreled into the Gulf of Mexico and plowed across Florida.
item.dc:creator: Wyatte Grantham-Philips And David Koenig, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This privacy and compliance threat throws shade at iPhone Mirroring
item.description: Apple-focused IT admins using device management software should perhaps temporarily disable iPhone Mirroring across their device fleets to prevent inadvertent privacy or compliance challenges as a result of using the new macOS Sequoia feature. (Apple is supposed to be working on a fix already.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Waiting for JD: Vance’s appearance at Silicon Valley fundraiser keeps nearby crowd on edge
item.description: Admissions to the event- the host was not clear - were going for up to $50,000 and the cheapest ticket costing $3,000.
item.dc:creator: Caelyn Pender, The Mercury News

item.title: How NTT DATA CIO Barry Shurkey takes a people-first approach to IT success
item.description: As CIO at NTT DATA North America, Barry Shurkey is responsible for digital transformation and optimizing the IT roadmap to support the company and its clients.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use Java generics to avoid ClassCastExceptions
item.description: Generics are used in Java to allow types or methods to operate on objects of various types while providing compile-time type safety. Generics address the problem of ClassCastExceptions being thrown at runtime as a result of code that is not type safe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI says cybercriminals are using ChatGPT more to influence elections
item.description: OpenAI has seen a number of attempts where its AI models have been used to generate fake content, including long-form articles and social media comments, aimed at influencing elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a report on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Greene takes fire for remarks about weather
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) has again thrown herself at the center of controversy over her embrace of claims that the government and humans are “controlling” the severe weather as Hurricane Milton barrels toward Florida.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google could be forced to sell parts of its business if the DOJ gets its way
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to force Google to sell parts of its business in order to eliminate its online search monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: The Evolution of ChatGPT: From Chats to Canvas
item.description: OpenAI's ChatGPT 4o with Canvas revolutionizes the AI-infused assistant, adding a new dimension to its functionality.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Musk steps up Starlink access as Hurricane Milton approaches Florida
item.description: SpaceX and CEO Elon Musk ramped up the rollout of Starlink's direct-to-cell service for areas expected to be hit Wednesday by Hurricane Milton, marking the latest assistance efforts by the aerospace company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft brings Copilot AI tools to OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot is now available in OneDrive, part of a wider revamp of the company’s cloud storage platform. Microsoft highlighted several ways to use the generative AI (genAI) assistant in OneDrive in a blog post Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people
item.description: "We could view cybersecurity as an entry level problem, but it is probably more of an intermediate and advanced role problem," Keith Clement said. The post A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Office vacancy levels soar to record highs in biggest Bay Area markets
item.description: The Bay Area's three primary office markets, haunted by empty buildings, have reached forbidding new milestones of record-high vacancy levels, according to a grim new report.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Google scientists share Nobel Prize in chemistry for AI protein work
item.description: Two Google DeepMind scientists shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in chemistry with a US professor for their breakthrough research into proteins.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Linus Torvalds annoyed by passive language in commit messages
item.description: The latest release candidate of Linux 6.12 was announced over the weekend, promoting some comments about grammar from Linux founder Linus Torvalds.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Electron vs. Tauri: Which cross-platform framework is for you?
item.description: If there’s one thing developers agree on about cross-platform applications, it’s that it’s easier to create a GUI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript than to use one of the many cross-platform frameworks out there. Frameworks like Electron give you the best of both worlds.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: WebSockets under the hood with Node.js
item.description: WebSockets is a network communication protocol that enables two-way client-server communication. WebSockets are often used for applications that require instantaneous updates, using a persistent duplex channel atop HTTP to support real-time interactions without constant connection negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Furious officials say misinformation is harming hurricane response
item.description: The aftermath of Hurricane Helene has sparked a flood of misinformation online, with federal, state and local leaders sounding the alarm that it is complicating an already difficult recovery process in the South.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Judge rules Google must open Android app store to competitors
item.description: A federal judge ruled Monday that Google must open the Play Store to rival third-party Android app stores. U.S. District Judge James Donato ordered the tech giant to allow for the distribution of third-party app stores and platforms through its own Android app store for a period of three years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Swiss Police Investigation into Suicide Capsule Use Suspended - Assisted Dying Laws Under Review
item.description: Switzerland's permissive assisted suicide laws spark debate as police investigation into first use of 'Sarco' capsule is suspended.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to remotely fix family computer woes
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores how you can help troubleshoot a computer issue remotely on Windows or a Mac. There are a variety of options.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
